recipes.title: Fondue
recipes.description: Swiss communal pot of melted cheese with white wine and kirsch, scooped up with crusty bread on long forks.
steps: Rub the inside of a fondue caquelon with the cut garlic clove and discard.
Pour the white wine and lemon juice into the pot and bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat.
Add the cheeses a handful at a time, stirring constantly in a figure-eight pattern until melted and smooth.
Whisk the cornstarch with the kirsch and stir into the cheese to thicken.
Season with nutmeg and black pepper.
Set the caquelon over a low flame at the table and serve with cubes of crusty bread for dipping.

recipes.title: Raclette
recipes.description: Swiss melted cheese scraped from a half wheel onto boiled potatoes with pickles, charcuterie and onions.
steps: Scrub the potatoes and boil them in their skins in well-salted water for 20 to 25 minutes until tender; keep warm.
Slice the raclette cheese into 5 mm slabs sized to fit your raclette pans.
Arrange the charcuterie, cornichons and pickled onions on a platter.
Place a slice of cheese in each individual raclette pan and slide under the heating element.
Melt the cheese until bubbling and just starting to brown at the edges, about 3 to 4 minutes.
Pour the molten cheese over a hot potato and finish with pepper or paprika.
Eat with a slice of charcuterie and a few pickles, repeating with each round.

recipes.title: Rösti
recipes.description: Swiss crisp grated potato cake fried in butter until golden brown on both sides, eaten as breakfast or side.
steps: Boil the potatoes in their skins for 10 minutes so they are par-cooked, then chill for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Peel the cold potatoes and grate them coarsely into a bowl.
Season with salt and pepper and toss gently.
Heat half the butter and the oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Spread the grated potato evenly in the pan and press down lightly with a spatula.
Cook for 10 to 12 minutes until the underside is deep golden and crisp.
Slide the rösti onto a plate, invert back into the pan with the remaining butter, and cook the second side for 10 minutes.
Slide onto a serving plate and cut into wedges.

recipes.title: Älplermagronen
recipes.description: Swiss alpine macaroni baked with potatoes, cream and cheese, topped with crispy onions and served with apple sauce.
steps: Boil the cubed potatoes in salted water for 5 minutes, then add the macaroni and cook together until both are tender; drain.
Meanwhile, slowly fry the sliced onions in butter over low heat for 20 minutes until deeply golden and crisp.
Warm the cream and milk in a saucepan with the nutmeg and a generous pinch of salt and pepper.
Layer the potato and macaroni mixture in a buttered baking dish with most of the cheese.
Pour the cream mixture over and top with the remaining cheese.
Bake at 200°C for 15 minutes until bubbling and golden.
Top with the crispy onions and serve with cold apple sauce on the side.

recipes.title: Zürcher Geschnetzeltes
recipes.description: Zurich-style sliced veal in a creamy white wine and mushroom sauce, traditionally served with rösti.
steps: Pat the veal slices very dry, season with salt and pepper, and dust lightly with flour.
Heat the oil in a wide skillet over high heat and sear the veal in batches for 30 seconds per side; set aside.
Lower the heat and add the butter, shallots and mushrooms to the same pan; cook until golden.
Pour in the white wine and simmer until almost evaporated.
Add the stock and reduce by half, then stir in the cream and simmer to a coating consistency.
Return the veal and any juices and warm through; do not boil.
Finish with lemon juice and parsley and serve immediately with rösti.

recipes.title: Birchermüesli
recipes.description: Original Swiss soaked oat muesli with grated apple, lemon, nuts and yogurt, invented by Dr. Bircher-Benner.
steps: Soak the rolled oats in the milk for at least 30 minutes or overnight.
Stir the yogurt and honey into the soaked oats.
Toss the grated apples in lemon juice to keep them bright, then fold into the oats.
Stir in the raisins and most of the chopped hazelnuts.
Spoon into bowls and top with fresh berries and the remaining hazelnuts.

recipes.title: Cervelat
recipes.description: Swiss national sausage of beef and pork, traditionally grilled with the ends scored to curl into a crown.
steps: Use a sharp knife to make 4 to 6 deep cuts at each end of every cervelat without cutting all the way through.
Heat a charcoal grill or barbecue to medium-high.
Grill the sausages for 3 to 4 minutes per side until the scored ends curl outward and the skins are blistered.
Slice the bread and lay out the mustard, cornichons and onion as accompaniments.
Serve the grilled cervelats hot with bread and the condiments.

recipes.title: Bündner Nusstorte
recipes.description: Engadin Swiss caramelized walnut tart enclosed in buttery shortcrust pastry, a famous regional speciality.
steps: Rub the butter into the flour, sugar and salt until crumbly, add the egg, and bring together into a dough; wrap and chill for 1 hour.
Caramelize the filling sugar in a dry pan to deep amber, then carefully whisk in the warmed cream and honey.
Stir the walnuts into the caramel and cool to room temperature.
Roll two thirds of the dough and line a 24 cm tart tin, pressing into the sides.
Pour in the walnut filling and level it.
Roll the remaining dough into a lid, place over the filling and crimp the edges to seal.
Brush with egg wash, prick a few steam holes, and bake at 180°C for 35 to 40 minutes until deep golden.
Cool fully in the tin before slicing.

recipes.title: Tartiflette
recipes.description: Alpine baked dish from Savoy of potatoes, lardons and onions topped with melting reblochon cheese.
steps: Boil the sliced potatoes in salted water for 8 minutes until just tender, then drain.
Sauté the lardons in butter until golden, then add the onions and cook until soft.
Pour in the white wine and simmer until almost dry.
Stir in the cream and the cooked potatoes and season with salt and pepper.
Rub a baking dish with the cut garlic clove and tip in the potato mixture.
Lay the two halves of reblochon on top, rind side up.
Bake at 200°C for 20 to 25 minutes until the cheese is melted and golden.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving with a green salad.
